nodes.name: Coefficient Giving
nodes.blurb: The largest AI-safety philanthropy — program-based funds (technical AI safety, AI governance), backed chiefly by Dustin Moskovitz and Cari Tuna.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of CY2024 grants in the archived Open Phil grants DB, programs: Global Catastrophic Risks Capacity Building + Potential Risks from Advanced AI

nodes.thesis: Fund the research, institutions, and talent pipelines that make transformative AI go well; concentrated bets, program officers with real budgets.

apply.notes: RFPs per fund; general inquiries rolling

nodes.name: Survival & Flourishing Fund
nodes.blurb: S-process rounds recommending grants paid by Jaan Tallinn, Jed McCaleb, and allied donors — one of the largest x-risk funders.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of SFF 2025 rows from the public recommendations table (grant records recomputed)

apply.notes: annual s-process rounds; application windows announced on the site

nodes.name: Long-Term Future Fund
nodes.blurb: EA Funds' rolling small-grants fund for AI safety and x-risk — the field's default first check for individuals and small projects.

sources.title: public grants CSV

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of LTFF grant records CY2024 (latest complete year: 137 records, ≥30% of prior year's count)

nodes.name: Manifund
nodes.blurb: Regranting marketplace: named regrantors with $100k+ budgets fund AI-safety projects in public, writeups and all.

nodes.name: Schmidt Sciences
nodes.blurb: Eric and Wendy Schmidt's science philanthropy — three AI-safety programs: AI2050 fellowships (nomination-only), the $10M Science of Trustworthy AI program (27 projects incl. Bengio, Kolter), and the $10M multi-agent safety joint call.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: AI Safety Science program commitment at launch

apply.notes: Open: 'Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World' (with Google DeepMind, CAIF, ARIA, Google.org) — Tier 1 <=$300K, Tier 2 $300K-$1M, closes Aug 8 2026. Science of Trustworthy AI 2026 RFP closed May 17 (decisions summer 2026); AI2050 is nomination-based.

sources.title: Multi-agent safety joint call — portal (tiers, deadline)

nodes.name: Macroscopic Ventures
nodes.blurb: Swiss nonprofit (formerly CERR, then Polaris Ventures) treating cooperative AI as a primary route to reducing catastrophic risk. Seeded CAIF ($15M) and CMU FOCAL ($3M); plans to give up to $100M/yr.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of grants listed on the public grants page

sources.title: Macroscopic focus areas (cooperative AI)

nodes.name: Foresight Institute
nodes.blurb: Runs focused AI-safety grant rounds (~$3M/yr) with small fast checks — plus fellowships and workshops that seed the field.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: AI safety grants program rate per its public grant pages

apply.notes: periodic rounds — next window on the site

nodes.name: Future of Life Institute
nodes.blurb: Grantmaking plus advocacy on transformative-AI risk; also appears as a payer in SFF rounds.

apply.notes: themed RFPs open periodically

nodes.name: NSF
nodes.blurb: US federal science funding; the Safe Learning-Enabled Systems program (co-funded with Coefficient Giving) is the flagship AI-safety vehicle.

apply.notes: PESOSE (NSF 26-506) — secure open-source ecosystems incl. AI agent protocol security; Track 1 <=$300K/1yr, Tracks 2-3 <=$1.5M/2yr; deadline Sept 1 2026. SaTC 2.0 (NSF 25-515) — 'Cybersecurity and AI' explicit priority, RES track <=$1.2M/4yr, next window Sept 28 2026. SLES program continues via standing solicitations.

sources.title: NSF Awards API
NSF PESOSE solicitation (deadline 2026-09-01)
NSF SaTC 2.0 solicitation

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of field-relevant NSF awards CY2024 (latest complete year: 24 records; SLES+keyword slice — not all of NSF)

nodes.name: UK AISI — Alignment Project
nodes.blurb: UK government-led international coalition funding alignment research — £27M round 1 across 60+ projects, backed by Schmidt Sciences, AWS, Anthropic and others.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Alignment Project round 1 total £27M at 1.27 USD/GBP

apply.notes: round 1 closed; reopens summer 2026

nodes.name: ARIA — Safeguarded AI
nodes.blurb: UK ARIA's £59M Safeguarded AI programme — mathematically verifiable safety guarantees, £2–3.5M per team calls. Scaling Trust (~£50M) targets safe large-scale ecosystems of interacting AI agents.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: £59M programme budget over its ~4-year life at 1.27 USD/GBP (estimate)

apply.notes: Opportunity Seeds: up to £500k/project; Safeguarded AI TA rounds announced separately

sources.title: ARIA Scaling Trust — multi-agent coordination funding

nodes.name: OpenAI (grant programs)
nodes.blurb: Cybersecurity Grant Program — a $1M grant program across 28 projects (incl. prompt-injection work) — plus $10M in API credits via Trusted Access for Cyber.

apply.notes: Defensive cybersecurity only ($10K increments, API credits and/or direct funding). Superalignment Fast Grants ended with the team's dissolution (May 2024). Researcher Access Program: rolling, up to $1K API credits.

sources.title: OpenAI: cybersecurity grant program status

nodes.name: Anthropic (fellows & access)
nodes.blurb: Fellows Program cohorts (funding + mentorship, incl. AI security) and researcher compute/API access.

apply.notes: cohort applications open per intake

nodes.name: AI Safety Fund (Frontier Model Forum)
nodes.blurb: The frontier labs' pooled $10M+ safety fund — rounds in 2024 and 2025 funding independent safety research.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: initial pooled commitment ($10M+) at launch

apply.notes: Meridian Institute (original administrator) closed June 2025; FMF self-administers. Two rounds completed (Nov 2024, Dec 2025); next round unannounced.

sources.title: AISF — administrator wind-down note

nodes.name: Menlo Ventures
nodes.blurb: Runs the $100M Anthology Fund with Anthropic — the most explicit VC vehicle for the AI/agent ecosystem, security startups included.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Anthology Fund commitment (fund total, not annual — attribution upper bound)

apply.notes: pitch anytime; Anthology targets AI-native startups

nodes.name: Sequoia Capital
nodes.blurb: Generalist tier-1 that has moved decisively into AI security — co-led Irregular's $80M Series B.

sources.title: Irregular $80M round coverage

nodes.name: Insight Partners
nodes.blurb: Growth investor that led Promptfoo's $18.4M Series A — one of the clearest bets on agent-security tooling.

nodes.name: FAR.AI
nodes.blurb: Frontier-AI safety research nonprofit with $30M+ multi-funder support.

nodes.name: Redwood Research
nodes.blurb: AI control and alignment research org; long-standing Coefficient Giving grantee.

nodes.name: METR
nodes.blurb: Frontier-model autonomy and dangerous-capability evaluations.

nodes.name: Apollo Research
nodes.blurb: Deception and scheming evals for frontier models.

nodes.name: EleutherAI
nodes.blurb: Open-source AI research lab — interpretability, open models, and the infrastructure the open safety ecosystem runs on.

nodes.name: MATS
nodes.blurb: ML Alignment Theory Scholars — the field's main research-talent pipeline.

nodes.name: Center for AI Safety
nodes.blurb: Research plus field-building (compute cluster, benchmarks, statements) on societal-scale AI risk.

nodes.name: Timaeus
nodes.blurb: Developmental interpretability research org.

nodes.name: Cooperative AI Foundation
nodes.blurb: Funds cooperative AI research: multi-agent safety, coordination, and human-AI cooperation. Seeded with $15M from Macroscopic Ventures; co-funder of the $10M 'Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World' call.
nodes.thesis: Supporting the foundational scientific research needed to understand, evaluate, and control risks emerging from large-scale ecosystems of interacting AI agents, deployed by multiple actors; advancing cooperative intelligence for the benefit of all.

apply.notes: Standalone CAIF grant rounds announced periodically (2025 round closed). Open now: the joint 'Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World' call with Schmidt Sciences, Google DeepMind, ARIA, Google.org — Tier 1 <=$300K, Tier 2 $300K-$1M, closes Aug 8 2026. Annual PhD fellowship (next cycle TBA).

nodes.blurb: Carnegie Mellon's Foundations of Cooperative AI Lab.

nodes.name: Irregular
nodes.blurb: AI security startup — raised an $80M Series B co-led by Sequoia and Redpoint.

nodes.name: Promptfoo
nodes.blurb: LLM red-teaming and eval tooling; $18.4M Series A led by Insight Partners — acquired by OpenAI in March 2026.

nodes.blurb: Runs the Manifund regranting marketplace.

nodes.blurb: Leads Foresight's grants, fellowships, and workshops.

nodes.name: Anthony Aguirre

nodes.blurb: Leads FLI's grantmaking and advocacy.

nodes.name: Lewis Hammond
nodes.title: Research Director
nodes.blurb: Research Director — Cooperative AI Foundation.

nodes.name: Cecilia Elena Tilli
nodes.title: Associate Director, Research and Grants
nodes.blurb: Associate Director, Research and Grants — Cooperative AI Foundation.

nodes.name: Machine Intelligence Research Institute
nodes.blurb: MIRI pioneered technical AI alignment research (decision theory, agent foundations) and has since pivoted to policy advocacy focused on halting superintelligence development through internationally coordinated moratoriums.

nodes.name: Jaan Tallinn
nodes.blurb: Skype/Kazaa co-founder; the largest individual payer behind SFF's s-process rounds.

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: sum of SFF 2025 rows paid by Jaan Tallinn

nodes.name: Center for Security and Emerging Technology
nodes.blurb: CSET is a Georgetown University policy think tank that produces data-driven analysis on the national-security implications of AI — covering compute/semiconductor supply chains, US-China AI competition, and AI applications in defense — to inform policymakers on AI governance.

nodes.name: 80,000 Hours
nodes.blurb: 80,000 Hours is a nonprofit that builds the AI safety field by providing career research, one-on-one advising, and a job board to direct talented people into high-impact AI safety roles across technical research, policy, and operations.

nodes.name: RAND Corporation
nodes.blurb: RAND Corporation is a global nonprofit policy research organization whose AI-safety-relevant work (sized here by its AI governance and safety grants received) spans compute governance, catastrophic-risk policy, AI safety institute design, and tabletop exercises on AI-enabled threats.

nodes.name: University of California, Berkeley
nodes.blurb: UC Berkeley hosts CHAI (Center for Human-Compatible AI, founded by Stuart Russell) and BAIR, conducting foundational AI safety research in value alignment, interpretability, and robustness (sized here by its AI-safety-relevant grants received).

nodes.name: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
nodes.blurb: MIT (sized here by its AI-safety-relevant grants received) hosts the CSAIL Algorithmic Alignment Group on technical alignment, the MIT AI Risk Initiative maintaining a 1,700+ risk taxonomy used by the UN and EU AI Office, and the AI Agent Index evaluating safety transparency in deployed agents.

nodes.name: Berkeley Existential Risk Initiative
nodes.blurb: BERI provides operational, financial, and administrative support (HR, grant management, compliance) to university AI-safety and x-risk research groups such as CHAI, FHI, and SERI, enabling them to focus on research rather than bureaucracy.

nodes.name: Lightcone Infrastructure
nodes.blurb: Lightcone Infrastructure builds and maintains LessWrong and the AI Alignment Forum — the primary online platforms for AI safety and rationalist research discussion — alongside the Lighthaven event venue in Berkeley, providing core community infrastructure for the field.

nodes.name: Epoch
nodes.blurb: Epoch AI is a research nonprofit that tracks AI training compute, model capabilities, and hardware investment trends to forecast transformative AI timelines and inform AI safety strategy for grantmakers and policymakers.

nodes.name: BlueDot Impact
nodes.blurb: BlueDot Impact is a non-profit talent accelerator that runs AI safety courses and career-transition programs to build the pipeline of professionals working on beneficial AI, having trained 7,000+ people at labs, government agencies, and AI safety organizations since 2022.

nodes.name: Stanford University
nodes.blurb: Stanford University conducts AI safety research through the Stanford Center for AI Safety and Stanford HAI, spanning technical alignment, trustworthy AI systems, and policy/governance — sized here by its AI-safety-relevant grants received.

nodes.name: Centre for the Governance of AI
nodes.blurb: GovAI is an independent nonprofit that conducts rigorous research on AI policy, threat modeling, and institutional risk management to help governments and decision-makers navigate the transition to advanced AI.

nodes.name: OpenMined
nodes.blurb: OpenMined is a nonprofit building open-source privacy-preserving AI infrastructure (PySyft/Syft) for federated computation across private datasets without centralizing data, with AI-safety-relevant work on privacy-preserving model evaluations (NIST partnership) and AI governance policy (UK Frontier AI Taskforce, U.S. Senate AI roadmap).

nodes.name: Center for a New American Security
nodes.blurb: CNAS is a nonpartisan national security think tank (sized here by its AI-safety-relevant policy work) that researches AI governance frameworks, international stability risks from advanced AI, and federal model assessment standards for the DoD and intelligence community.

nodes.name: European Summer Program on Rationality
nodes.blurb: ESPR is a free 10-day residential camp in the UK for mathematically talented 16–19 year olds that teaches rationality, AI safety, and AI evaluation to build the pipeline of future alignment-aware researchers.

nodes.name: Jie Yang
nodes.title: Program Officer, CPS-Cyber-Physical Systems, AI-Safety
nodes.blurb: NSF Program Officer — CPS-Cyber-Physical Systems, AI-Safety

nodes.title: Program Officer, AI-Safety
nodes.blurb: NSF Program Officer — AI-Safety

nodes.title: Program Officer, AI-Safety
nodes.blurb: NSF Program Officer — AI-Safety

nodes.name: Christopher Yang
nodes.title: Program Officer, AI-Safety
nodes.blurb: NSF Program Officer — AI-Safety

nodes.name: AI Risk Mitigation Fund
nodes.blurb: The AI Risk Mitigation (ARM) Fund, a project of Effective Ventures Foundation spun out of the Long-Term Future Fund in December 2023, makes grants to reduce catastrophic AI risks through technical alignment research, AI policy work, and programs that recruit new researchers into AI safety.

apply.notes: Currently doing proactive grantmaking only; no open application portal as of mid-2026. Prospective grantees can email info@airiskfund.com to express interest. Site states no set date for open applications.

sources.title: AI Risk Mitigation Fund — official site
AI Risk Mitigation Fund — About (team page)
AI Risk Mitigation Fund — Grants

nodes.thesis: Reduce catastrophic risks from advanced AI through grants towards technical research, policy, and training programs for new researchers — AI companies alone are not enough, with strikingly few researchers working on key safety problems outside major industry labs.

nodes.name: Caleb Parikh
nodes.title: Program Associate
nodes.blurb: Program Associate — AI Risk Mitigation Fund.

nodes.name: Oliver Habryka
nodes.title: Program Associate
nodes.blurb: Program Associate — AI Risk Mitigation Fund.

nodes.name: Longview Philanthropy
nodes.blurb: Longview Philanthropy is an EA-aligned nonprofit grantmaker that has directed over $204 million toward AI risk reduction as of June 2026, funding technical safety research, policy development, and field-building through its public Emerging Challenges Fund and private Frontier AI Fund.

apply.notes: Emerging Challenges Fund is a public grantmaking program; the private Frontier AI Fund is for donors giving over $100,000 and is invite-access only.

sources.title: Artificial Intelligence - Longview Philanthropy
AI Safety and Alignment Grant Program (2026) | Granted AI
Michael Aird - Longview Philanthropy

nodes.thesis: Private markets systematically underinvest in managing AI risks because profits concentrate among a few companies with little incentive to prioritize societal interests over short-term gains; philanthropy can fill this gap by advancing cutting-edge technical research on alignment and interpretability, shaping governance frameworks, and building the field of AI safety.

nodes.name: Michael Aird
nodes.title: AI Program Director
nodes.blurb: AI Program Director — Longview Philanthropy.

nodes.name: Aidan O'Gara
nodes.title: AI Program Officer
nodes.blurb: AI Program Officer — Longview Philanthropy.

nodes.name: Founders Pledge (GCR)
nodes.blurb: Founders Pledge's Global Catastrophic Risks Fund makes philanthropic grants to high-impact organizations addressing AI safety policy, governance, and resilience — alongside biosecurity and nuclear risk — prioritizing hits-based policy advocacy and field-building in underinvested areas.

apply.notes: Fund primarily identifies grantees through its own research and expert networks; external expressions of interest can be submitted via the requests-for-funding page, though that page's stated criteria skew toward development programs. No formal rounds or deadlines.

sources.title: Global Catastrophic Risks Fund — Founders Pledge
Celebrating a decade of impact: Reducing Global Risks — Founders Pledge 2024
Building global resilience to AI risks — Founders Pledge

nodes.thesis: "Reduce the probability of large-scale catastrophic events; mitigate the potential negative impacts of these events if they occur; improve the ability to anticipate new and emerging risks on the horizon." The fund addresses AI risks through diplomatic confidence-building measures, government detection and response capacity, and resilience-building interventions it considers relatively neglected compared to technical alignment work.

nodes.name: Christian Ruhl
nodes.title: Global Catastrophic Risks Lead
nodes.blurb: Global Catastrophic Risks Lead — Founders Pledge (GCR).

nodes.name: Halcyon Futures
nodes.blurb: Halcyon Futures is a nonprofit incubator and early-stage fund that backs founders in AI safety, biosecurity, and critical cybersecurity through grants up to $1M, venture investments, and career transition support.

apply.notes: Apply via founders page or hello@halcyonfutures.org; no formal deadline; seeks seasoned founders, senior operators, technical researchers, and policy/defense veterans

sources.title: Halcyon Futures – Official Site
About Halcyon Futures
Halcyon Futures Inc – ProPublica Nonprofit Explorer (EIN 93-3537739)

nodes.thesis: The single biggest bottleneck to addressing these risks is exceptional founders and leaders.

nodes.name: Mike McCormick
nodes.title: Founder & CEO
nodes.blurb: Founder & CEO — Halcyon Futures.

nodes.name: Nick Raushenbush

nodes.name: Safe AI Fund
nodes.blurb: Early-stage venture fund that writes $100K SAFEs into startups building tools for AI safety, security, interpretability, and responsible deployment; founded April 2025 by former Y Combinator president Geoff Ralston.

apply.notes: Applications accepted on an ongoing basis via the Request Funding form on saif.vc; no fixed deadline.

sources.title: SAIF - Safe Artificial Intelligence Fund (official site)
Former Y Combinator president Geoff Ralston launches new AI safety fund — TechCrunch
Accelerating Safely — Geoff Ralston Substack

nodes.thesis: "Help exceptional founders build products that support a future where AI is both powerful and aligned with human interests" — a bet on a future that is both accelerated and aligned, not a reaction to hype or fear.

nodes.name: Geoff Ralston
nodes.title: Founder
nodes.blurb: Founder — Safe AI Fund.

nodes.name: Nick Ralston
nodes.title: Partner
nodes.blurb: Partner — Safe AI Fund.

nodes.name: Craig Newmark Philanthropies
nodes.blurb: Craig Newmark Philanthropies is the personal philanthropy of craigslist founder Craig Newmark, which has committed $100 million to a Cyber Civil Defense initiative funding cybersecurity workforce development, tools development, and critical infrastructure protection.

apply.notes: Accepts unsolicited proposals via online form with PDF attachment; brief proposals preferred; proposals supporting military families or veterans should apply through the Bob Woodruff Foundation portal instead

sources.title: Cybersecurity | Craig Newmark Philanthropies
Apply | Craig Newmark Philanthropies
$3.2 million from Craig Newmark Philanthropies accelerates IST's UnDisruptable27 effort

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Stated $100M commitment to Cyber Civil Defense network launched in 2022

nodes.thesis: Cybersecurity is a modern patriotic duty — a "whole-of-society" cyber civil defense modeled on historical civil defense, where citizens, nonprofits, and institutions actively participate to protect critical infrastructure, power grids, and connected devices from nation-state and criminal cyber threats.

nodes.name: DARPA
nodes.blurb: DARPA's Information Innovation Office (I2O) funds high-risk AI security and cybersecurity R&D through office-wide BAAs covering trustworthy AI, resilient software, and offensive/defensive cyber capabilities, and previously ran the AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC) prize competition ($29.5M total prizes, concluded Aug 2025).

apply.notes: DICE (HR001126S0010, IPTO) — decentralized AI agent collectives with controlled emergence; PM Susmit Jha; proposals due 2026-08-25 14:00 ET. MATHBAC (math foundations of agent communication) Phase I closed June 2026. I2O office-wide BAA (HR001126S0001): rolling abstracts until Nov 1 2026 covering trustworthy AI and resilient secure software.

sources.title: AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC) | DARPA
AI Cyber Challenge marks pivotal inflection point for cyber defense | DARPA
AI Forge: Accelerating AI breakthroughs for national security | DARPA
DARPA I2O Office-Wide BAA HR001126S0001 details
DARPA DICE program page (deadline 2026-08-25)
DARPA MATHBAC — math foundations of agent communication

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Total prize pool committed for AI Cyber Challenge (AIxCC) two-year competition (2023–2025), including $7M reserved for small businesses

nodes.thesis: AI that is significantly more reliable and predictable in high-stakes settings, understandable to its operators, and secure in contested environments — achieved through AI interpretability, verifiable human control, and adversarial robustness research.

nodes.name: IARPA
nodes.blurb: IARPA funds competitive multi-year R&D programs for the U.S. Intelligence Community on AI security, including trojan/backdoor detection in deployed models (TrojAI, completed 2025) and LLM threat quantification and mitigation (BENGAL, active).

apply.notes: IARPA funds research via competitive Broad Agency Announcements (BAAs) posted on iarpa.gov and sam.gov. TrojAI BAA (2019) and BENGAL BAA (deadline Jan 29, 2024) are both closed; performer teams have been selected. Monitor open-baas page for new AI-security solicitations.

sources.title: IARPA TrojAI Program
Intelligence Community AI Cybersecurity Program Achieves Massive Scientific Impact (AFCEA)
Trojans in Artificial Intelligence (TrojAI) Final Report (2026)
IARPA Advances BENGAL Program (MeriTalk)

nodes.thesis: "To defend AI systems from intentional, malicious attacks, known as Trojans, by conducting research and developing technology to detect these attacks in a completed AI system" and "to understand the landscape of LLM threats and vulnerabilities, with the goal of developing novel technologies to analyze and address these shortcomings, enabling the Intelligence Community to safely harness LLM technology across a broad range of critical applications."

nodes.name: Kristopher Reese
nodes.title: TrojAI Program Manager
nodes.blurb: TrojAI Program Manager — IARPA.

nodes.name: Tim McKinnon
nodes.title: BENGAL Program Manager
nodes.blurb: BENGAL Program Manager — IARPA.

nodes.name: NIST (CAISI)
nodes.blurb: NIST's Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) — successor to the U.S. AI Safety Institute — conducts voluntary evaluations of AI capabilities for national security risks and develops security guidelines and standards for AI on behalf of the U.S. government.

apply.notes: The NIST AI Consortium (the membership body through which CAISI conducts collaborative research) accepts letters of interest from all organizations that can contribute expertise, products, data, or models — open on an ongoing basis with biannual review periods; first review began June 2026. Participants enter a CRADA with NIST. No funding is provided — this is a collaborative research arrangement, not a grant program. CAISI does not operate a public grant program.

sources.title: Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) | NIST
What Will It Cost for the US to Be Ready for the Next Big AI Breakthrough? | IFP
NIST Expands AI Consortium's Scope, Calls for New Members | NIST

aliases: National Institute of Standards and Technology
nodes.thesis: CAISI serves as "industry's primary point of contact within the U.S. government to facilitate testing and collaborative research related to harnessing and securing the potential of commercial AI systems," focusing on evaluations covering cybersecurity, biosecurity, and chemical weapons risks.

nodes.name: UKRI EPSRC
nodes.blurb: EPSRC (part of UKRI) co-funds the Systemic AI Safety Grants Programme (£8.5m total commitment) with Innovate UK, with the AI Safety Institute managing applications; Phase 1 supported approximately 20 projects at up to £200k each on systemic risks from AI in critical infrastructure and societal systems.

apply.notes: AI safety-specific calls run as competitive rounds co-administered with AISI. Phase 1 of the Systemic AI Safety Grants Programme closed November 2024; ~20 projects funded at up to £200k each. Future rounds expected but none announced as of July 2026. Standard EPSRC research grants accept applications on a rolling basis year-round.

sources.title: Advancing the field of systemic AI safety: grants open
£54 million boost to develop secure and trustworthy AI research
UK Launches £200,000 Grants for Systemic AI Safety

aliases: Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: £8.5m total programme commitment for Systemic AI Safety Grants Programme (EPSRC + Innovate UK), converted at 1.27 USD/GBP

nodes.thesis: Develop an initial understanding of systemic AI safety risks and identify promising mitigations that can be promoted in future programme phases; the programme targets risks that arise at a systems level — in critical infrastructure and societal systems — rather than from individual model capabilities alone.

nodes.name: Allison Eberz
nodes.title: EPSRC Senior Portfolio Manager, AI & Robotics
nodes.blurb: EPSRC Senior Portfolio Manager, AI & Robotics — UKRI EPSRC.

nodes.name: EU Horizon
nodes.blurb: Horizon Europe is the EU's main R&I funding programme (2021–2027, €93.5B total) that issues competitive calls for AI security and trustworthy AI research; the dedicated SecureAI call (HORIZON-CL3-2026-02-CS-ECCC-02, €21.2M) specifically targets hardening AI systems against adversarial attacks, backdoor injections, and data poisoning in compliance with the EU AI Act.

apply.notes: SecureAI (HORIZON-CL3-2026-02-CS-ECCC-02) — EUR 21.2M for adversarial robustness, data poisoning, backdoor exploitation, federated-learning security; deadline 2026-09-15 17:00 CEST; EUR 3-4M per project, up to 5 funded; min. 3 partners from 3 eligible countries.

sources.title: HORIZON-CL3-2026-02-CS-ECCC SecureAI call — ECCC
New ECCC call for proposals under Horizon Europe open for applications — ECCC 2026-03-13
Horizon Europe — European Commission Research and Innovation

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Budget for HORIZON-CL3-2026-02-CS-ECCC-02 (SecureAI) call in the 2026 Horizon Europe work programme; EUR 21.2M converted at ~1.09 USD/EUR

nodes.thesis: "Strengthen the resilience of AI systems and algorithms against various threats and attacks" — proposals should develop real-time anomaly detection, mitigation techniques to defend against adversarial attacks, and robust federated learning techniques, in synergies with leading efforts on AI transparency and in compliance with the AI Act.

nodes.name: Ballistic Ventures
nodes.blurb: Cybersecurity-dedicated early-stage VC ($660M across two funds) that backs AI security and agentic-security startups from seed through Series A.

apply.notes: Pitch via info@ballisticventures.com.

sources.title: Ballistic Ventures — official site
Ballistic Ventures Closes $360M Fund II (PR Newswire, 2024)
Ballistic Ventures fund profile — VCSheet (check size data)

nodes.thesis: "The most important action we can take today is to fuel the ideas of the world's greatest cybersecurity entrepreneurs with our collective experience, network, and passion." Ballistic is dedicated to funding and incubating cybersecurity innovations exclusively, co-founding and incubating startups alongside operators who have lived the founders' challenges.

nodes.name: SYN Ventures
nodes.blurb: SYN Ventures is a cybersecurity-specialist VC that backs early-stage companies across enterprise security — with a growing cluster of AI agent security investments including Arcade (AI agent governance), CodeIntegrity (AI agent runtime control), and Helmet Security (agentic AI communications security).

apply.notes: Submit pitch via online form on contact page. Flagship fund leads late-stage Seed, Series A, and B rounds for cybersecurity companies with enterprise traction; Seed fund leads or co-leads Seed rounds.

nodes.thesis: Focused on investing in disruptive and innovative companies in the cybersecurity, industrial security, national defense, privacy, regulatory compliance, and data governance industries.

nodes.name: YL Ventures
nodes.blurb: Israeli cybersecurity-exclusive seed VC ($800M AUM across five funds) that backs early-stage Israeli founders building category-defining security companies, with focus areas including AI-enhanced security, non-human identity security, and cloud security.

apply.notes: No formal application portal; founders contact via LinkedIn or website. Focuses exclusively on Israeli-founded cybersecurity companies at seed stage.

sources.title: YL Ventures — From Seed to Scale (official site)
YL Ventures Identifies Key Cybersecurity Sectors set to Dominate in 2025
The next big exits in Israeli AI cyber? YL Ventures is betting on resilience, not hype

nodes.thesis: "Dedicated exclusively to cybersecurity" — partners with top Israeli founders from inception, "helping build category-leading companies that define the market" through domain expertise, hands-on company building, and access to CISOs and industry leaders; backs founders who "design with resilience" for real and urgent security problems.

nodes.name: Yoav Leitersdorf

nodes.name: Cyberstarts
nodes.blurb: Cyberstarts is a seed-stage cybersecurity VC that bills itself as the 'day one partner for industry-defining cybersecurity companies,' with a portfolio including AI security plays such as Oasis (AI agent identity) and Cyera (data security), managing $1.5B in assets across seven funds.

apply.notes: VC pitching on a rolling basis; firm engages founders before product or team exists, typically invite-driven through their CISO advisory network.

sources.title: Cyberstarts — Official Site
Cyberstarts Closes Second $380M Opportunity Fund
Cyberstarts launches $380M fund to double down on cybersecurity scale-ups

nodes.thesis: "The day one partner for industry-defining cybersecurity companies" — identifying founders before products exist by focusing on those who have confronted substantial adversity ('Hunting for adversity'), paired with a proven operational playbook and an advisory network of CISOs.

nodes.name: Gili Raanan
nodes.title: Founder
nodes.blurb: Founder — Cyberstarts.

nodes.name: Dor Knafo
nodes.title: General Partner
nodes.blurb: General Partner — Cyberstarts.

nodes.name: Andreessen Horowitz
nodes.blurb: Andreessen Horowitz is a major US venture capital firm that backs AI-native security startups—covering AI-powered threat detection and social engineering prevention—through direct equity investments and its Speedrun seed accelerator.

apply.notes: Speedrun accelerator (up to $1M, 12-week in-person SF program) runs in cohort rounds. SR007 deadline was May 17 2026 and is now closed; next cohort TBD. Standard VC portfolio: pitch a partner directly at any time.

sources.title: Breaking the Cybersecurity Kill Chain with AI
Investing in Adaptive Security
a16z Speedrun FAQ

nodes.thesis: "AI-native security is the platform shift of today. Just as next-generation firewalls were not simply 'firewalls++,' AI-native security requires rebuilding prevention, detection, and response so that intelligence and automation are the foundation rather than a plug-in."

nodes.name: Zane Lackey
nodes.title: General Partner
nodes.blurb: General Partner — Andreessen Horowitz.

nodes.name: Joel de la Garza
nodes.title: Partner
nodes.blurb: Partner — Andreessen Horowitz.

nodes.name: Redpoint Ventures
nodes.blurb: Multi-stage VC (seed through growth) that co-led Irregular's $80M Series A in September 2025 to secure frontier AI models, and actively tracks AI security — non-human identity and AI SOC automation — as investment thesis areas within its infrastructure practice.

apply.notes: No explicit pitch submission process described on the website; homepage lists a 'Join the Redpoint network' submission form whose purpose is not specified.

sources.title: Irregular Raises $80M to Secure the Future of AI with Frontier Security Lab | Redpoint Ventures
Irregular raises $80M to secure frontier AI models | TechCrunch
Sai Senthilkumar | Redpoint Ventures

nodes.thesis: Redpoint backs bold builders challenging industry conventions with technical depth and conviction to build enduring $1B+ companies; within its infrastructure practice it tracks non-human identity security and AI SOC automation as distinct investment thesis areas.

nodes.name: Jordan Segall
nodes.title: Partner (early-stage)
nodes.blurb: Partner (early-stage) — Redpoint Ventures.

nodes.name: Sai Senthilkumar
nodes.title: Partner (growth)
nodes.blurb: Partner (growth) — Redpoint Ventures.

nodes.name: Google DeepMind
nodes.blurb: Google DeepMind funds external AI safety research through grant programs and co-funded calls, including a $10M multi-agent AI safety funding call (2026) co-led with Schmidt Sciences, ARIA, Google.org, and the Cooperative AI Foundation, targeting technical safety research on large-scale agent ecosystems.

apply.notes: Co-funder of 'Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World' with Schmidt Sciences, CAIF, ARIA, Google.org — $10M total; Tier 1 <=$300K (1-2 yr), Tier 2 $300K-$1M (1-2 yr). Deadline Aug 8 2026 AoE per the smapply portal (ARIA's page says Aug 9; portal is primary). Decisions autumn 2026.

sources.title: Google DeepMind and partners announce multi-agent safety research funding call
Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World — Cooperative AI Foundation grants page
Responsibility & Safety — Google DeepMind
Multi-agent safety joint call — application portal (tiers, deadline)

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Up to $10M committed to the Scaling AI Safety for a Multi-Agent World funding call, co-funded with Schmidt Sciences, ARIA, Google.org, and Cooperative AI Foundation — announced June 11 2026

nodes.thesis: "AI can provide extraordinary benefits, but like all transformational technology, it could have negative impacts unless it's developed and deployed responsibly." DeepMind's safety strategy is to "anticipate and evaluate our systems against a broad spectrum of AI-related risks, taking a holistic approach to responsibility, safety and security," including funding distributed global research to identify emergent risks in multi-agent systems before they scale.

nodes.name: Helen King
nodes.title: VP, Responsibility
nodes.blurb: VP, Responsibility — Google DeepMind.

nodes.name: Microsoft (AFMR)
nodes.blurb: Microsoft Research's Accelerating Foundation Models Research (AFMR) Responsible AI track provided access to Azure-hosted foundation models and cloud resources to academics studying AI alignment, adversarial robustness, prompt injection defenses, and interpretability; the program has concluded and its successor is the Agentic AI Research and Innovation (AARI) initiative.

apply.notes: AFMR has concluded; pages are maintained for archival reference. Successor AARI program is not open for general applications — the current page shows only pre-selected project collaborations.

sources.title: AFMR: Responsible AI - Microsoft Research
Accelerating Foundation Models Research - Microsoft Research
Agentic AI Research and Innovation - Microsoft Research

nodes.thesis: "Align AI with shared human goals, values, and preferences via research on models which enhances safety, robustness, sustainability, responsibility, and transparency"

nodes.name: Peter Lee
nodes.title: Corporate Vice President, Microsoft Research & Incubations
nodes.blurb: Corporate Vice President, Microsoft Research & Incubations — Microsoft (AFMR).

nodes.name: Ade Famoti
nodes.title: Senior Director, Microsoft Research (leads AARI successor program)
nodes.blurb: Senior Director, Microsoft Research (leads AARI successor program) — Microsoft (AFMR).

nodes.name: AWS Programs
nodes.blurb: Amazon Web Services funds AI-security and agentic-AI research through the Amazon Research Awards (ARA) program — unrestricted grants averaging $70K–$80K plus AWS Promotional Credits to academic institutions — and committed $2.5M to the Linux Foundation's Alpha Omega initiative to help open-source maintainers manage AI-generated vulnerability reports.

apply.notes: Spring 2026 cycle closed May 13, 2026; decisions expected August 2026. Fall cycle typically opens around October but no date has been published as of July 3, 2026. Separate rolling AWS Cloud Credit for Research program also available at aws.amazon.com/grants.

sources.title: AI for Information Security Call for Proposals – Spring 2026 – Amazon Science
AWS Agentic AI Call for Proposals – Spring 2026 – Amazon Science
AWS and Others Invest $12.5M to Defend the Open Source Ecosystem from AI Threats – AWS Open Source Blog

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: AWS committed $2.5M of a $12.5M joint investment with Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI into Alpha Omega (Linux Foundation) to help open-source maintainers validate and remediate AI-generated vulnerability reports, announced March 17, 2026.

nodes.thesis: Awards are structured as unrestricted gifts to principal investigators' academic institutions, with no IP transferred to Amazon and recipients encouraged to publish openly and contribute to open-source repositories.

nodes.name: Greylock
nodes.blurb: Led Braintrust's seed (AI eval infra, Saam Motamedi) and 7AI's $36M seed (agentic security, Asheem Chandna); 80%+ of its checks are first-money pre-seed to Series A into AI-first companies.

apply.notes: No open application; pitch the sector partner directly — Saam Motamedi for AI infra/evals, Asheem Chandna or Jason Risch for security. Firm positions itself as first-check investor, so approach at pre-seed/seed.

sources.title: Greylock — AI investing focus page
Greylock: Infrastructure for AI's New Era (Braintrust investment post)
Braintrust seed round announcement (Greylock led)
SecurityWeek: 7AI raises $36M seed for agentic security platform

nodes.thesis: Greylock says it is 'focused on AI-first companies,' betting that foundation models create 'models that can write, code, draw, and act on users' behalf' across AI applications, infrastructure, and foundation models. Over 80% of its investments are the first check at pre-seed, seed, or Series A.

nodes.name: Saam Motamedi

nodes.blurb: Partner (AI, cybersecurity, data infrastructure) — Greylock.

nodes.name: Lightspeed Venture Partners
nodes.blurb: Led Anthropic's $3.5B Series E as largest investor; AI-security portfolio incl. Cyera (seed-stage backer, now $12B), Descope (IAM for AI agents), BlinkOps (security micro-agents). Publishes the Cyber60 landscape.

apply.notes: No open application; pitch via the relevant partner — Guru Chahal for identity/agent-security infra (Descope memo co-author), Ravi Mhatre for frontier AI. Firm claims 'over a decade as AI founders' first call'; has deployed $5.5B+ across 165+ AI-native startups.

sources.title: Lightspeed announces lead investment in Anthropic's $3.5B Series E
Lightspeed: Doubling down on Descope — IAM for customers, partners, and AI agents
BusinessWire: BlinkOps raises $50M Series B (Lightspeed existing investor)
Lightspeed team page

nodes.thesis: Lightspeed's Anthropic memo states 'the biggest value from AI's development will be unlocked at the frontier' and that 'trust is the foundation upon which all AI adoption is built.' Its Cyber60 report frames AI as 'the biggest threat in cybersecurity, and also our greatest hope,' flagging non-human identity for AI agents as a priority gap.

nodes.title: Partner
nodes.blurb: Partner — Lightspeed Venture Partners.

nodes.name: Ravi Mhatre

nodes.blurb: Partner & Co-Founder — Lightspeed Venture Partners.

nodes.name: Accel
nodes.blurb: Backs Oasis Security ($120M Series B, non-human-identity security covering AI agents), led Surf AI's $40M Series A in agentic security ops, and co-led Cursor/Anysphere's $2.3B round at $29.3B.

apply.notes: No open application; pitch a partner with AI+Security focus (page lists focus areas per investor — e.g., Casey Aylward, Steve Loughlin, Sara Ittelson, Ping Li at early stage). 'Prepared mind' firm: expect them to know your category's research already.

sources.title: Accel: Solving the non-human identity security gap — our investment in Oasis
Accel: Context is all you need — our cyber investment in Surf AI
TechFundingNews: Accel and Sequoia back Oasis Security's $120M Series B for secure AI agents
Accel people page

nodes.thesis: Accel's Oasis investment post argues machine identities 'now outnumber human ones by almost 50x' and enterprises need 'a centralized system for governing non-human identities'; its Surf AI post holds that 'the limiting factor for cybersecurity teams is execution, not detection' — agents should fix, not just flag, within explicit guardrails.

nodes.name: Casey Aylward
nodes.title: Investor, Early Stage — AI, Cloud/SaaS, Security

nodes.name: Index Ventures
nodes.blurb: Led 7AI's $130M Series A (Dec 2025) — billed as the largest cybersecurity Series A in history — with Shardul Shah joining the board; also backs agent platforms Wonderful ($100M Series A), Hebbia, and Harvey.

apply.notes: No open application; pitch Shardul Shah for security/agentic-security (led 7AI, on its board; NYC, Midas List). Firm mantra: 'We invest in who you are, not just what you do' — founder-story-first pitches.

sources.title: Index Ventures: Security's Agentic Era Starts Here — our investment in 7AI
SecurityWeek: Agentic security firm 7AI raises $130 million
Index Ventures team page
Index Ventures: Wonderful secures $100M to drive adoption of AI agents globally

nodes.thesis: Index's investment post 'Security's Agentic Era Starts Here' frames autonomous SOC agents as the start of security's agentic era, with 7AI's agents completing investigations in minutes and 'Fortune 500 customers experiencing measurable results at scale within weeks.' Related posts back identity and human-risk security 'for the agentic era' (NewCore, Frame).

nodes.name: Shardul Shah
nodes.title: Partner
nodes.blurb: Partner — Index Ventures.

nodes.name: Bessemer Venture Partners
nodes.blurb: Published 'Securing AI agents: the defining cybersecurity challenge of 2026' (Atlas, Mar 2026); AI-security portfolio incl. Bold Security (AI-first DLP), Clarity (deepfake defense), Doppel, and Axonius.

apply.notes: Unusually direct route: the Atlas post publishes partner emails and invites AI-security founders to reach out — Amit Karp (karp@bvp.com), Mike Droesch (mdroesch@bvp.com), Yael Schiff, Elliott Robinson.

sources.title: BVP Atlas: Securing AI agents — the defining cybersecurity challenge of 2026
BVP cybersecurity roadmap and portfolio
BVP portfolio: Bold Security (AI-first DLP, partnered 2024, led by Amit Karp)
BVP team page

nodes.thesis: BVP's Atlas thesis declares 'securing AI agents has become the defining cybersecurity challenge of 2026' — agents' 'capability and exposure scale together' — and lays out a visibility → configuration → runtime-protection roadmap, calling targeted in-flight intervention the clearest open infrastructure opportunity.

nodes.name: Conviction
nodes.blurb: Sarah Guo's $230M AI-native 'Software 3.0' fund; early/first checks into agent companies Sierra ($15B), Cognition (Devin), Harvey ($11B raise), and Mistral. Writes $1-25M checks, often as first investor.

apply.notes: Pitch Sarah Guo or Mike Vernal directly; for very early founders, Conviction also runs Embed (grant program for early-stage AI founders) as an on-ramp. Concentrated portfolio (~27 companies) — expect high conviction bar.

sources.title: Conviction homepage (thesis, team, portfolio, check size)
TechCrunch: Guo's Conviction Partners adds Mike Vernal as GP, raises $230M fund
Forbes: Sarah Guo bet everything on AI pre-ChatGPT (portfolio valuations)

nodes.thesis: Conviction describes itself as 'a new venture firm purpose-built to serve AI-Native, Software 3.0 companies,' believing 'we are extremely early in the translation of powerful AI models to powerful products that transform industries.' It invests $1-25M at pre-seed through Series A, 'often the first investor.'

nodes.title: Founder & General Partner
nodes.blurb: Founder & General Partner — Conviction.

nodes.title: General Partner
nodes.blurb: General Partner — Conviction.

nodes.name: AI Safety Tactical Opportunities Fund
nodes.blurb: Pooled multi-donor fund managed by JueYan Zhang on Manifund. >$30M raised, 150+ grants in governance, technical alignment, and AI evals/auditing. Fast-moving; recent $437K grant for AI collaboration infrastructure.

apply.notes: Submit a project via the Create button on Manifund (manifund.org). No formal application portal; the route in is listing a project on the Manifund platform for regranting consideration by AISTOF.

sources.title: AISTOF — Manifund profile
AISTOF grant history on Manifund

nodes.thesis: "A pooled multi-donor charitable fund seeking to reduce the probability and severity of catastrophic risks associated with the development of advanced AI systems" — structured to be fast and capture emerging opportunities in a rapidly changing landscape.

nodes.name: JueYan Zhang
nodes.title: Fund Manager
nodes.blurb: Fund Manager — AI Safety Tactical Opportunities Fund.

nodes.name: Catalyze Impact
nodes.blurb: Global AI safety founder incubator. Seed Funding Circle channels $50K-$300K per org; Winter 2024/25 cohort launched 11 new orgs in London. ~2 pitch rounds/yr, 3-6 org proposals per round.

apply.notes: Apply via Airtable form on catalyze-impact.org. Organizations must first go through Catalyze's selective incubation program before accessing the Seed Funding Circle. Pitch rounds run ~2x/yr with 3-6 org proposals per round.

sources.title: Catalyze Seed Funding Circle
Catalyze Program Details
Catalyze: Introducing 11 New Ventures — Winter 2024/25 cohort

nodes.thesis: "Founders we support want to create solutions before things break" — Catalyze provides direction, co-founder matching, mentorship, and seed funding to founders building AI safety and resilience organizations before critical systems fail.

nodes.name: Alexandra Bos
nodes.title: Co-Founder & CEO
nodes.blurb: Co-Founder & CEO — Catalyze Impact.

nodes.name: Vitalik Buterin
nodes.blurb: Individual donor; May 2021 gave $665M face-value Shiba Inu crypto to FLI (already on this map — money flows through FLI). His $25M program funds PhD/postdoc AI existential-safety fellows via FLI+BAIF; next cycle expected fall 2026.

apply.notes: Individual donor — no direct application portal. His giving flows through the Vitalik Buterin Fellowships in AI Existential Safety, run by FLI and BAIF. 2026 PhD cycle closed Nov 21, 2025; postdoc cycle closed Jan 5, 2026. Next cycle expected fall 2026.

sources.title: FLI: Vitalik Buterin PhD Fellowships
FLI: Finances — Buterin as largest donor
Wikipedia: Future of Life Institute — $25M grant program

nodes.thesis: Funds early-career researchers tackling AI existential risk through named FLI/BAIF fellowship programs; concerned that without deliberate safety work, AI could become a dominant and uncontrollable force.

nodes.name: Laude Institute
nodes.blurb: Founded June 2025 by Andy Konwinski ($100M personal pledge). Slingshots = fast grants for open-source/CS research; Moonshots = multi-year labs. Framed around beneficial-AI, not alignment per se. First grant: $15M to UC Berkeley.

apply.notes: Applications via 'Request' link on laude.org. Slingshots: fast, low-friction grants for researchers commercializing work or building open-source infrastructure. Moonshots: multi-year, multi-million dollar seed grants for long-horizon challenges.

sources.title: Laude Institute
Laude Institute: Hello, World launch post
TechCrunch: Konwinski pledges $100M on Laude Institute

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Andy Konwinski's stated $100M personal commitment at institute launch, June 2025; first grant $3M/yr x 5 years to UC Berkeley AI Systems Lab

nodes.thesis: "Breakthrough ideas matter, but only if you ship them" — Laude operates at the intersection of academia and industry to fund researchers who bridge paper-publishing and real-world deployment.

nodes.name: Juniper Ventures
nodes.blurb: AI-safety-native VC with pre-seed, growth, and foundation vehicles. Portfolio: Goodfire, Apollo Research, Gray Swan, Andon Labs. Backers incl. Reid Hoffman, Jan Leike, Woj Zaremba. Fund size undisclosed.

apply.notes: Reach out via team@juniperventures.xyz; no formal portal. Three vehicles: Juniper Ventures (pre-seed), Juniper Growth (Series A-C), and Juniper Foundation (later stage).

sources.title: Juniper Ventures
VC Spotlight: Juniper Ventures
DNV Ventures invests in Juniper Ventures Fund II

nodes.thesis: Invests in "assurance infra" — security, evals, interpretability, inference, compliance, and governance — backing founders building AI systems that are "secure and beneficial for humanity."

nodes.name: Benchmark
nodes.blurb: Led Manus AI's $75M round (autonomous agents, later acquired by Meta ~$2B); early backer of Sierra (enterprise AI agents) and Cursor. Concentrated early-stage bets; $2B new funds raised June 2026.

apply.notes: No public application portal. Benchmark proactively sources deals; warm introductions via portfolio founders strongly preferred.

sources.title: TechCrunch: Benchmark raises first-ever growth fund in $2B capital raise
TechCrunch: Manus gets Benchmark funding at $500M valuation

nodes.thesis: Benchmark concentrates on a small number of early-stage companies where a GP takes an active board seat, targeting seed through Series B in AI agents, enterprise software, and developer tools.

nodes.blurb: Led Exaforce's $75M Series A (agentic SOC — 'Exabots' for security triage/response); backed Cognition (Devin), Parallel, and OpenAI (2019 entry + $405M SPV in 2024).

apply.notes: Contact via kv@khoslaventures.com. No public pitch portal; warm introductions preferred.

sources.title: Khosla Ventures portfolio
Exaforce $75M Series A press release (Khosla led)
Khosla Ventures team

nodes.thesis: Khosla Ventures takes contrarian, high-conviction early bets under the mantra 'venture assistance, not just venture capital'; its AI focus spans foundation models, agentic enterprise software, and AI-native security operations.

nodes.name: Thrive Capital
nodes.blurb: Largest institutional OpenAI backer (~$1B+ across rounds at $29B, $150B, and $285B valuations); co-led Cursor's $2B round at $50B valuation (2026, with a16z). Also backed Scale AI and Physical Intelligence.

apply.notes: No public pitch portal. Thrive targets companies demonstrating strong growth; warm introductions strongly preferred.

sources.title: CNBC: Thrive Capital backs OpenAI at $285B valuation
The Information: a16z and Thrive co-lead Cursor at $50B valuation

nodes.thesis: Thrive builds and invests in internet, software, and technology-enabled companies; its AI portfolio reflects deep multi-stage conviction in frontier labs and AI-native developer tools, cemented by a mutual equity exchange with OpenAI in December 2025.

nodes.name: Joshua Kushner
nodes.title: Founder & Managing Partner
nodes.blurb: Founder & Managing Partner — Thrive Capital.

nodes.name: South Park Commons
nodes.blurb: Pre-seed community + fund (1000+ members); portfolio incl. Cognition, Imbue, and Goodfire (mechanistic interpretability). $1M-$10M first checks; 6-month no-equity residency track open to researchers.

apply.notes: Rolling admissions; 1-3 week response time. Two tracks: Residency (6 months, no cost, no equity, open to researchers and pre-idea builders) and Founder Fellowship (for those ready to fundraise immediately).

sources.title: South Park Commons Fund
South Park Commons portfolio
South Park Commons team

nodes.thesis: SPC helps founders go from -1 to 0 — a no-equity 6-month residency for pre-idea exploration, plus a fund writing $1M-$10M first checks before traction, targeting ambitious founders and researchers at the frontier of AI.

nodes.name: AI Grant
nodes.blurb: Nat Friedman & Daniel Gross's program: ~90 companies over 4 batches incl. Perplexity, Cursor, ggml/llama.cpp, Haize Labs (AI safety evals), Goodfire. $250K SAFE + $350K Azure credits. Dormant since founders joined Meta mid-2025.

apply.notes: Closed. Batch 4 applications closed with no Batch 5 announced; founders Nat Friedman and Daniel Gross joined Meta Superintelligence Labs mid-2025. Program managed for existing portfolio only — no new investments.

sources.title: AI Grant — official site (Batch 4 closed)
SaaStr: the $1.1B VC fund that 4x'd then got acquired by Meta

nodes.thesis: AI Grant backed technical AI founders with $250K uncapped SAFEs plus $350K in Azure credits per company across 4 cohorts (2023-2024), targeting frontier AI tooling, open-source infrastructure, and agents.

nodes.name: Alfred P. Sloan Foundation
nodes.blurb: Funds AI-in-science transparency and open-source research software via $100-400K rolling grants. Metascience & AI Postdoc Fellowships target reproducibility. Relevant to academic open-source AI and human-AI interaction.

apply.notes: Submit a two-page letter of inquiry to program email; no fixed deadlines on any program page. Separate LOI channel for Open Source in Science via same staff.

sources.title: Sloan: Exploratory Grantmaking in Technology
Sloan: Open Source in Science
Sloan: Metascience & AI Postdoctoral Fellowships

nodes.thesis: "To continually monitor the landscape of emerging technology with the aim of identifying emerging new sectors, tools, and methods of working that we believe have potential, but which may not yet receive funding from mainstream/other sources." The Open Source in Science program develops tools, norms, and institutions for open-source research software, hardware, and models.

nodes.title: Program Director, Digital Technology
nodes.blurb: Program Director, Digital Technology — Alfred P. Sloan Foundation.

nodes.name: Evan S. Michelson

nodes.name: Mozilla
nodes.blurb: Open-source AI accelerator (up to $100K; funded Llamafile, SQLite-vec) plus $50K-$250K Democracy x AI cohort for accountability tools; seeded ROOST open safety infra. Mozilla's main vehicle for open-source AI grantmaking.

apply.notes: Builders 2024 accelerator cohort (14 projects, up to $100K) ended December 2024; no 2025/2026 cohort announced. Democracy x AI 2026 cohort closed March 16, 2026 (current cohort began June 2026); sign up for future cohorts via mozillafoundation.org. MOSS on indefinite hiatus since 2020.

Democracy x AI Cohort — Mozilla Foundation

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: Democracy x AI cohort: $1M total over 24 months per PR Newswire announcement = ~$500K/year; Builders accelerator disbursed ~$1.4M in 2024 but has no recurring published commitment

nodes.thesis: "Mozilla Builders helps developers create transformative open-source AI projects through collaborations, programming, and community." The Democracy x AI cohort funds researchers and technologists building tools to strengthen democratic institutions against AI-driven manipulation.

nodes.name: NLnet Foundation
nodes.blurb: EUR 5-50K grants for open internet infrastructure, privacy tech, and AI commons every ~2 months via NGI programs (EUR 21.6M budget). Fits open-source agent-trust protocols, evals tooling, and safety infrastructure.

apply.notes: Active calls: NGI TALER (EUR 676K pot) and NGI Fediversity (EUR 450K pot), both close August 1, 2026 12:00 CEST. NGI Zero Commons Fund final call closed June 1, 2026; regular open calls resume after summer 2026.

NGI Fediversity open call — NLnet

nodes.thesis: NLnet provides grants to independent software engineers and researchers working on technology commons — including privacy-friendly AI and open infrastructure — from EUR 5,000 to EUR 50,000 "with the possibility to scale them up significantly if there is proven potential."

nodes.name: Office of Naval Research
nodes.blurb: Funds cooperative swarm autonomy, multi-agent coordination, and human-machine teaming through the CAST program and Long Range BAA (rolling, open through Sept 2026). YIP awards $750K/3yr to early-career researchers.

apply.notes: Long Range BAA N00014-25-S-B001 (Amendment 0007, May 2026) accepts rolling proposals through 2026-09-30 14:00 EST. Research proposals via grants.gov; contract proposals via submissions.nre.navy.mil. YIP FY2027 cycle expected to open ~Oct 2026.

ONR 2026 Young Investigator Program (~$17M, 25 awardees)

nodes.thesis: ONR's mission is to "deliver critical, cutting-edge capabilities to the Sailors and Marines" through global scientific partnerships. Research in multi-agent autonomy, swarm theory, and human-machine teaming is funded as a direct warfighter capability pathway.

nodes.blurb: Program Officer, Machine Learning Reasoning and Intelligence (Code 311) — Office of Naval Research.

nodes.name: DoD MURI
nodes.blurb: Joint ONR/ARO/AFOSR program funding 5-year university teams (~$7.5M/team; FY2025: 26 teams, $200M). Recent topics: AI safety of autonomous systems, ethical adaptive AI, generative-AI evaluation.

apply.notes: FY2026 solicitation closed September 2025. FY2027 solicitation not posted as of 2026-07-05; prior cycles suggest solicitation ~Feb-March, white papers ~May, full proposals ~September.

sources.title: ONR MURI program page
FY2025 MURI solicitation — incl. Topic 26, safety of intelligent autonomous systems
FY2026 MURI solicitation — incl. ethical adaptive AI, gen-AI evaluation topics
DoD grants $200M for FY2025 MURI (26 teams)

nodes.thesis: "DoD's MURI program addresses high-risk basic research and attempts to understand or achieve something that has never been done before," funding multi-investigator university teams to produce breakthroughs with far-reaching consequences.

nodes.name: CIFAR (Canadian AI Safety Institute)
nodes.blurb: Canadian federal AI-safety program (C$27M ~= US$19.7M total). Catalyst Grants up to C$100K/yr; Solution Networks up to C$700K/2yr. 18 projects in year 1; C$1M committed to the global Alignment Project coalition.

apply.notes: No open calls as of 2026-07-05. Second Catalyst cohort (sociotechnical track, 8 projects) announced March 2026; that deadline was November 2025. Solution Networks EOIs closed. Monitor cifar.ca/ai/caisi/ for future calls.

sources.title: CIFAR CAISI program page
Government of Canada announces C$27M CAISI Research Program
CIFAR second Catalyst cohort (8 projects, March 2026)
CIFAR commits C$1M to the Alignment Project

annualFieldGivingBasis.method: C$27,000,000 total program commitment (announced Nov 2024) x 0.73 CAD/USD

nodes.thesis: "Canadian-led, multidisciplinary research to tackle both immediate and long-term safety challenges posed by advanced AI systems"; combines rapid-response Catalyst Grants with longer-term Solution Networks.

nodes.name: NAIRR Pilot
nodes.blurb: NSF-led infrastructure giving U.S. researchers GPU compute, cloud credits, datasets, and models at no cost. Startup track: up to 2,000 GPU-hrs/3mo with 3-week turnaround; full allocations reviewed monthly.

apply.notes: Full allocations: submit by the 15th of each month; decisions by end of the following month. Apply at submit-nairr.xras.org with an ACCESS ID. Startup track (~2,000 GPU-hrs/3mo): 3-week turnaround. U.S.-based institutions only.

sources.title: NAIRR Pilot portal
NAIRR startup allocation (2,000 GPU-hrs/3mo)
NSF NAIRR overview (14 federal agencies, 30+ industry partners)

nodes.thesis: The NAIRR Pilot's mission is to democratize AI research by "connecting U.S. researchers and educators to AI compute, data, software, models, training, and educational materials," reducing infrastructure barriers that concentrate AI R&D at large institutions.
